_meta.purpose: Authoritative data dictionary. The LLM uses this to map natural-language questions to fields, allowed values, and operations. Anything not described here is OUT OF SCOPE and must return 'no data available'.
_meta.study_area: City of Tempe, Maricopa County, Arizona, USA

school_crashes_tempe.csv.description: Every Tempe crash tagged with nearest school, distance, and engineered time/condition features. Primary table for the chatbot — has coordinates for mapping.

crash_data_tempe.csv.description: Raw ADOT/MCDOT crash records with full attributes (street names, injury severity, driver demographics, violations).

feature_table_tempe.csv.description: Model-ready engineered features (KSI label, time parts, distances, road class).

school_crash_summary_tempe.csv.description: Per-school aggregated crash and KSI counts (precomputed). Use for school ranking questions.

schools_tempe_clean.json.description: Verified Tempe-area school points (GeoJSON). 159 names verified, 29 carry district/organization names. Use for plotting schools and re-running the school-crash join.

crash_hour.description: Hour of day (0-23). Use for 'most dangerous time' questions.

is_dark.description: 1 = dark lighting condition at time of crash.

KSI.description: 1 = crash resulted in a killed or seriously injured outcome. Core severity metric.

Injuryseverity.description: Reported injury severity. 'Fatal' and 'Incapacitating/Suspected Serious' map to KSI.

values: Sleet Hail Freezing Rain Or Drizzle
ANGLE (Front To Side)(Other Than Left Turn)

JunctionRelation.description: Relationship of crash to a junction/intersection.

Unittype_Two.description: Type of second unit (null = single-unit crash).

example_values: Failed To Yield Right Of Way
Violation1_Drv1.description: Primary violation by driver 1. Many distinct values.

Age_Drv1.description: Age of driver 1. NOTE: 255 and values <14 are data errors — filter out.

speed_limit_proxy.description: PROXY speed limit derived from road class — NOT actual posted limit. Caveat in answers.

StreetName.description: Primary street of crash. Use for 'crashes on X street'.

CrossStreet.description: Cross street. Combine with StreetName for intersection queries.

nearest_school.description: Name of nearest school within 1.0 mi. 'Unknown' = no school within 1 mile (11,704 rows). Use with dist_school_mi for radius questions.

dist_school_mi.description: Distance from crash to nearest school in miles. Capped at 1.0. Use for '0.25 mi radius' filters.

map_contract.note: Any query returns the matching rows' lat/long so results can be plotted (clustered markers + optional school buffer).

out_of_scope.rule: If a question cannot be answered from the fields above, respond exactly: 'No data available for that.'
examples: Cities other than Tempe (Phoenix, Mesa, Chandler, Scottsdale)
Future predictions / forecasts (the ML model is separate, not wired into chat in v1)
Specific individuals' identities, names, license plates
Pedestrian/bike counts or traffic volume per road (AADT not yet joined)
Actual posted speed limits (only a proxy exists)
Causal claims ('what causes crashes') — only patterns/correlations are reported

data_quality_notes: Several categorical columns contain stray numeric codes (e.g. '51', '255', '10') — treat as 'Unknown' / filter out.
Age_Drv1 includes sentinel 255 and implausible low values — restrict to 14-100 for age questions.
nearest_school 'Unknown' (11,704 rows) means no school within 1.0 mi, NOT a missing name. Re-running the join with schools_tempe_clean.json (and optionally a wider radius) is needed to reduce it.
'Counts' reflect crash frequency, not risk per traffic volume. State this when answering 'most dangerous' questions.
Only police-reported crashes are included; minor incidents undercounted.
